text:
Admission Date:  2018-03-17     Discharge Date:  2009-11-06

Date of Birth:                  Sex:

Service:

This is a transfer of this patient from U-Ville for elective
bronchoscopy.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS: The patient is a 51 year old
female with post polio syndrome, Crohn's disease and breast
cancer. The patient was admitted to Dominguez Marian Center Hospital
on 1977-02-19 to 1978-12-14 with pneumonia.  Hospital course was
complicated by intubation secondary to respiratory failure.
She failed to wean from the ventilator and underwent
tracheostomy and percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy
placement.  Also, she had a pleural effusion drained. She was
transferred to U-Ville on 1978-12-14.

The patient has been experiencing increasing shortness of
breath and cough over the past week.  She underwent
bronchoscopy on 1990-02-09 and was noted to have irregularities
of the left middle main stem bronchi, electively admitted to
the Medical Intensive Care Unit for further evaluation of
this abnormality.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:  Significant for tracheostomy placed in
01-18.  Percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy tube placed
01-18.  History of post polio syndrome.  History of Crohn's
disease.  History of breast cancer, 1998.  Status post
lumpectomy and XRT on Tamoxifen.  History of low back pain.

MEDICATIONS ON ADMISSION:
Combivent.
Alprazolam 3 mls q h.s.
Ferrous sulfate 325 mg p.o. q. day.
Folic acid one q. day.
Floxetene 20 mg q. day.
Heparin 5,000 units twice a day subcutaneous.
Loperamide 4 mg three times a day.
Magnesium oxide 800 mg three times a day.
Ranitidine 150 mg twice a day.
Tamoxifen 20 mg q h.s.
Trazodone 300 mg q h.s.
Tylenol.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:  Temperature of 100.4; blood pressure
121/63; heart rate 85.  She is on pressor support ventilation
10/5, FI02 of 0.35%, 250 times 24, 48%.  Pupils are equal,
round, and reactive to light and accommodation.  Lungs:
Coarse breath sounds bilaterally.  Regular rate and rhythm.
No murmurs, rubs or gallops.  Abdomen soft, nontender, no
distention. Bowel sounds positive.  Extremities:  No
clubbing, cyanosis or edema.  Central nervous system:  Alert
and oriented times three.

SOCIAL HISTORY:  She lives with her husband and daughter.
Christopher Campbell.  [**Name2 (NI) **] tobacco, no alcohol.

FAMILY HISTORY:  An aunt with breast cancer.  Parent with
colon cancer.

LABORATORY DATA:  On admission, white blood cell count was
10.5; hematocrit of 29.5; platelets 284.  Sodium 139;
potassium of 4.6; chloride of 96; C02 of 35; BUN 10;
creatinine 0.1; glucose of 92.  2.1 and 5.1.

HOSPITAL COURSE:  The patient underwent elective bronchoscopy
which the patient tolerated without complications. There was
no marked irregularity seen of the main stem bronchus on
bronchoscopy.  She then proceeded to CT of the chest to
further evaluate this lesion.  The CT was read as no
abnormality and only moderate stenosis of the bronchus.  It
was not felt that stenting would be of benefit in a lesion as
moderate as this.

The rest of the systems were inactive during this
hospitalization.  Hospital course was uneventful.  The
patient was transferred back to U-Ville in good condition on
2009-11-06.  No medication changes were made during this
hospitalization.

DISCHARGE DIAGNOSES:
Post polio syndrome.
Status post tracheostomy.
Status post percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy placement.
Respiratory difficulty secondary to post polio syndrome.  No
interventions indicated at this time.


                          Roberto Murphy Amanda Pollard, M.D.  79797

Dictated By:Sanchez
